title: padus serotina neomontana
intro: Padus serotina neomontana, also known as the Chokecherry or May Cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and fruits of Padus serotina neomontana are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
